matcher.subject: Science (086)
matcher.coveragePreview: chemical reactions and equations - session 1 grade 10 science students chemical reactions and equations signs of chemical change writing balanced equations law of conservation of mass application chemical reactions and equations session overview what makes a change 'chemical'? from words to symbols: writing equations the rule of balance is it a new substance? signs of chemical change physical change: reversible, same substance (e.g., ice melting) chemical change: irreversible, new substance formed (e.g., iron rusting) key question: can we get the original back easily? how do we know? (4 signs) signs of chemical change: gas evolution, color change, precipitate formation, temperature change 1. evolution of gas (bubbles) 2. change in color 3. formation of a precipitate (solid from liquids) 4. temperature change (heat released or absorbed) from words to symbols reactants and products notation writing skeletal equations reactants (left) -> products (right) use chemical symbols/formulas arrow indicates 'yields' or 'forms' example: hydrogen + oxygen -> water why must equations be balanced? law of conservation of mass balanced vs unbalanced equations law: mass is conserved in chemical r...
